text: Which pattern ensures a class has only one instance?
The Observer pattern is primarily used for?
options: Creating objects without specifying their class
Wrapping an object to add behaviour

text: Which pattern separates the construction of a complex object from its representation?
The Strategy pattern belongs to which GoF category?
Which pattern provides a unified interface to a set of interfaces in a subsystem?
What is the key difference between Decorator and Proxy?
options: Decorator adds behaviour; Proxy controls access
Proxy adds behaviour; Decorator controls access
They are identical patterns with different names
Decorator is structural; Proxy is creational

text: In the Template Method pattern, which part is defined in the base class?
options: The algorithm skeleton; subclasses fill in specific steps
Nothing — all logic lives in subclasses

text: Which pattern is used by Java's Collections.unmodifiableList()?
The Visitor pattern is best suited when?
options: You need to add operations to classes without modifying them, and the structure is stable
You want to decouple sender and receiver
You need lazy initialisation of expensive objects

text: Which pattern does the Hollywood Principle ('don't call us, we'll call you') describe?
What is the intent of the Factory Method pattern?
options: Create a single shared instance of a class
Define an interface for creating an object, letting subclasses decide which class to instantiate
Build complex objects step by step
Provide a family of related objects without specifying concrete classes

text: What problem does the Adapter pattern solve?
options: Adding new behaviour to an object at runtime
Allowing incompatible interfaces to work together
Simplifying a complex subsystem behind a single interface

text: Which pattern does Java's Iterable / for-each loop implement?
The Command pattern is most useful when you need to?
options: Notify multiple objects when state changes
Encapsulate a request as an object to support undo, queuing, or logging
Reduce the number of objects by sharing common state
Define the skeleton of an algorithm in a base class

text: What is the key difference between Factory Method and Abstract Factory?
options: Factory Method uses inheritance to create one product; Abstract Factory uses composition to create families of related products
Abstract Factory creates a single product; Factory Method creates families of products
Factory Method is a class pattern; Abstract Factory is an object pattern — they solve the same problem
Abstract Factory always uses a static method; Factory Method does not

text: Which pattern is used by servlet filter chains and Spring Security's filter pipeline?
The Composite pattern is best used when?
options: You need to add behaviour to individual objects without modifying their class
You need to treat individual objects and compositions of objects uniformly through a tree structure
You need a single point of access to a subsystem
You need to pass a request along a chain of handlers

text: The State pattern is primarily used to?
options: Cache and restore an object's internal state
Allow an object to alter its behaviour when its internal state changes, appearing to change its class
Separate the algorithm from the object that uses it

text: What is the key difference between Adapter and Bridge?
options: Adapter is designed upfront to decouple abstraction from implementation; Bridge retrofits two incompatible interfaces
Bridge is designed upfront to decouple abstraction from implementation; Adapter retrofits two incompatible interfaces
They are the same pattern — Bridge is the GoF name, Adapter is the common name
Adapter works with classes; Bridge works with objects only

text: java.lang.Integer.valueOf(127) returns the same object on repeated calls, but Integer.valueOf(1000) may not. Which pattern explains this?
Spring's ApplicationContext creates and wires beans on your behalf. Which pattern best describes this?
options: Singleton — all beans are singletons by default
Factory Method — the container uses factory methods to instantiate beans
Service Locator — callers look up dependencies from a registry
Dependency Injection via a Factory — the container acts as a Factory that resolves and injects dependencies
